issues.comment: Args corruption on DB retry in `updateDevice`. The `args` slice is declared outside the closure but mutated inside it via `args = append([]interface{}{query}, args...)`. On the first call, `args` becomes `[query, clientIP, userAgent, ...]`. On a retry (triggered by lock contention), `args` is already `[query, clientIP, ...]`, and the prepend makes it `[query, query, clientIP, ...]`. The `Exec` call then treats the second `query` string as the first SQL parameter value, shifting all parameters by one and passing one extra — this will produce a database error or silently wrong results.

Missing integration test skip guard. Every other `TestIntegration*` function in this file calls `testutil.SkipIntegrationTestInShortMode(t)` as the first line. The new `TestIntegrationBeyondDeviceLimit` test omits this call, meaning the integration test will run during `go test -short` when it shouldn't, which is inconsistent with the codebase convention and could slow CI or fail in environments without a database configured.
